original text:
London Evening Standard - The New Optimism: How You Can Be Happy | Robert Holden, Ph.D.
Psychiatrists used to study misery.
Then one day Martin Seligman, the (now very wealthy) father of the positive psychology movement, had the bright idea of turning things on their heads.
Rather than study unhappy people, he thought, why not look at happy people?

original text:
A Sip of Bliss | Page 3 of 12 | Celebrating Life's Everyday Bliss.
Today I am excited to be guest posting over at Simple Acres Blog.
I am sharing my recipe for spiced peanut butter yogurt dip.
This is one of my go to snacks, but with a bit of a fall twist with pumpkin pie spice.
This makes a perfect mid day work or after school snack or even breakfast and it great for adults and kids alike.
Head on over to Simple Acres Blog for the recipe!
These are super easy and have a wonderful sweet and spicy flavor.
They are great to use anywhere you would use regular pickles (sandwiches, burgers, etc.)
and are also great on their own.
Add sliced cucumbers and onions to a 32oz glass jar.
Bring all other ingredients to a boil in a medium saucepan and stir until sugar dissolves.
Pour the hot pickling liquid over the cucumbers and onions to fill the jar.
Allow to cool at room temperature then store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.
Today I am guest posting over at The Cup of Life with the DIY Green Tea After Sun Spray.
If you skin needs some refreshment this summer, head over to the blog and check it out!

Allow to cool at room temperature then store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.
Today I am guest posting over at The Cup of Life with the DIY Green Tea After Sun Spray.
If you skin needs some refreshment this summer, head over to the blog and check it out!

I hope you enjoy it and thanks for watching!
Categories: General Posts, Today's Word | Tags: Don Shomette, People are the Prize, Peoplearetheprize.com, Preventing Violence, Principals, School Resource Officer, SRO, The Shomette Group | Permalink.
Discipline: How Much Should An SRO Be Involved?
When should a building principal call the SRO to be involved in disciplinary issues?
We live in a contextual world and it helps to place tough decisions in a known context.
Here’s one technique (and context) that principals can use as a filter for when to call or not to call an SRO.

original text:
3 Take-Aways from 3 Days @BHI2017 | Health Solutions Research, Inc.
Both Ram and I presented papers at the IEEE International Conference on Biomedical & Health Informatics (BHI2017) conference that wrapped up earlier today in Orlando, FL and are happy to report that our talk our GeoHealth Dashboard was well received indeed.
My talk focused on the GeoHealth Dashboard we have built over the past year.
The GeoHealth Dashboard is a Geographic Information System (GIS) platform with a data analytic engine that takes health data, demographic data, as well as social factors impacting health outcomes to develop solutions for some of the most critical population health challenges we currently face.
Two specific applications for which the GeoHealth Dashboard provides insights were the subject of Ram’s talk – first how the Dashboard can provide decision support at both the hospital systems level and the state level into better fiscal management of Maryland’s All-Payer system.
Second, the Dashboard can also be used in developing responses to the opioid epidemic we currently face.
That our talks were well received is an indication that there is a lot of exciting around the prospect of using GIS to analyze data.
Natural Language Processing is garnering a lot of attention from the research community – highlighting that data entry challenges into electronic health record (EHR) systems are not only real, but do hamper the ability of those systems to contribute to patient care.
Another area of research focus at BHI2017 were the various efforts in machine learning, including deep learning approaches.
There is a great deal of interest in using machines and artificial intelligence to process large amounts of healthcare data to do a range of things from improving patient diagnosis to developing new standards of care.
Overall, BHI2017 was a good collection of engineers, scientists, and practitioners working to develop new technologies to advance healthcare into the future.
We’re glad to have been a part.
And the journey isn’t done – HIMSS17 starts tomorrow!

And the journey isn’t done – HIMSS17 starts tomorrow!

original text:
Some tips for dealing with a New York condo board of managers | Nadel & Ciarlo, P.C.
On behalf of Nadel & Ciarlo, P.C.
posted in Residential Real Estate on Tuesday, November 25, 2014.
Condominiums have become a popular form of residential real estate ownership for many New Yorkers.
In a condominium, residents have separate ownership of their individual units, and an undivided interest, shared with other residents, in the common areas such as hallways, elevators and building lobbies.
Condominiums are governed by a board of managers.
Unfortunately, with some boards this is not always the case.
A condo board is legally obligated to follow the internal rules and procedures found in the condominium declaration, the by-laws and the house rules.
Like the board of directors of a corporation, they are obligated to act with prudent judgment in making business decisions.
Boards are also bound to comply with the New York Condominium Act.
If you believe your board is not acting in compliance with internal rules or the law, your first step should be to speak to a board member, explain the problem and request that it be remedied or corrected.
If that does not resolve the issue, you should write a letter to the board and keep a copy for your records.
Your request will carry much more weight if you have the support of other residents.
Condominium boards are elected by the residents and will be more responsive to an organized group.
Litigation should be a last resort in a dispute with a condo board.
Litigation can be expensive and you should keep in mind that you have to live in the same building with the people you are suing.
Nonetheless, in serious cases where the board is not complying with the law or the internal rules of the building, it may be necessary to go to court.

Loosens truck cage bolt to unlock air brakes PLUS removes air brake booster.
For Bendix brakes that have a return spring hooking over a post.
The socket end removes springs by rotating the hook post off.
The opposite end leverages spring onto post.
Wind back disc brake pistons on Audi | Ford | Nissan | Peugeot | Rover | Subaru | VW vehicles.
Hooks the brake return spring.
Screw up to lock it for removal & installation.
A smart tool to service those brakes that don't use anchor pins.
Brake spoons are used for brake lining adjustment on drum brakes.
The special shape is designed to probe drum brake backing plates during adjustment.
Suits 8mm - 24mm washers.
Easy removal of brake shoe hold down springs.
Large window for easy visibility.
These pliers are designed to remove / replace brake springs on Bendix | Girling | Lockheed brakes.
One end of the handle has a socket type spring remover.
The other has a spoon end for replacing springs on anchor posts.
Curved neck provides easy access to hold down pins.
Universal application.
A specialised wrench to adjust the brakes on Hino #700 trucks.
This tool restores the brake caliper piston to its original resting position when servicing disc brakes.
Suits most popular vehicles.
A handy tool for driving brake rivets on trucks / tractors.
Simply securet the riveting jig in a vice.
Select the appropriate punch size and anvil to suit the brake rivet.
Hold the brake shoe and lining in one hand, and then fasten the rivet using a hammer.
Also suitable for older cars fitted with rivetted brake linings.
Disconnects quick connect push-in connectors on truck air systems.
Sizes: 1/4" | 3/8" | 1/2" | 5/8"
